# Development Plan: Issue #404

## Issue Summary

**Title**: [Storybook] Badges wall view (`BadgesWall`)
**Type**: feature (Storybook component issue, Epic #384 Track A)
**Complexity**: MEDIUM
**Estimated Lines**: ~500-520 hand-written (4 new files + i18n additions); pseudo-locale regeneration is auto-generated and called out separately, not hand-authored

## Intent Verification

- [x] When `spotlight` is non-null, the wall renders a "JUST EARNED" card with the spotlight badge's own shape/art, its goal title, and a formatted earned date; pressing it calls `onOpenBadge(spotlight.id)`. (Tested + visually confirmed.)
- [x] When `spotlight` is null (e.g. `SingleBadge`/`Empty` stories with 0 badges), no spotlight card renders. (Tested.)
- [x] The gallery renders **one `BadgeWallCell` per `gallery` item, with no cap** — a 20-item `gallery` array produces 20 rendered cells, not 12 + "+8 MORE" (D9, no `wall.moreCount` key exists anywhere in the diff). (Tested with `0/1/20`.)
- [x] No hamburger/sort affordance is rendered anywhere in `BadgesWall` (mockup-only per issue).
- [x] With `animationPref === "full"`, the spotlight glow (and the empty-state ghost-badge glow) loop continuously; with `"reduced"` or `"none"`, no loop starts and nothing pulses. (Tested via `withRepeat` call assertion.)
- [x] When `count === 0`, the wall renders the redesigned empty state ("Your first badge is waiting" / the finish-a-goal body copy / "See my goals →") instead of the old "No badges yet" copy, with a bespoke ghost-badge glyph (not the generic `EmptyState` icon prop). (Tested + visually confirmed.)
- [x] The `AllThemesMatrix` story renders the wall surface (bg + count text + spotlight card + a gallery cell) once per each of the 7 product themes (`light-default`, `dark-default`, `light-highContrast`, `light-dyslexia`, `light-autismFriendly`, `light-lowVision`, `light-lowInfo`), each column scoped via `ScopedTheme` and labeled with its mood name — legible in all 7 without a second hardcoded hex beyond the documented `#161616` fallback. (Visually confirmed across all 7.)
- [x] `BadgesWall` is not imported by `BadgesScreen.tsx` or any other screen at the end of this PR (container wiring is #405). (`BadgesScreen.tsx` untouched.)

## Dependencies

| Issue | Title                                       | Status                                         | Type    |
| ----- | ------------------------------------------- | ---------------------------------------------- | ------- |
| #403  | [Storybook] BadgeWallCell primitive + story | ✅ Met (closed 2026-06-29, merged via PR #418) | Blocker |

**Status**: ✅ All dependencies met.

Note: issue #404's GitHub labels still include `dep:blocked` as of this research pass — that's stale bookkeeping on the issue itself (the blocking condition it names, #403 closed, is satisfied); not a real blocker. Worth clearing the label when the issue is picked up, but it doesn't gate this plan.

## Objective

Ship `BadgesWall` — the pure, prop-driven "wall of proof" presentational view for the Badges tab (dark full-bleed surface, count header, most-recent-badge spotlight, dense all-badges gallery of `BadgeWallCell`s, redesigned empty state) plus its Storybook stories (including the 7-theme `AllThemesMatrix` check) and unit tests. No container/query/navigation wiring — that lands in #405. This is the direct continuation of #403 (which shipped the `BadgeWallCell` gallery primitive) and re-implements Step 5 of the #381 master plan against the real `BadgeWallCell` API (Step 5 in #381 was written against the older `BadgeCircleCell`, since superseded).

## Decisions

| ID  | Decision                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                               | Alternatives Considered                                                                                                                                                              | Rationale                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                   |
| --- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| D1  | `badgesWithGoalsQuery` is confirmed at `apps/native-rd/src/db/queries.ts:1262-1282` (not line 1198 as the issue body states), `ORDER BY badge.createdAt desc`, row shape `{ id, goalId, imageUri, design, createdAt, goalTitle, goalDescription, completedAt }`.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                       | Trust the issue's cited line number                                                                                                                                                  | The file has grown since the issue was written; line-number citations drift. Verified directly — content/behavior (`ORDER BY ... DESC`) matches what the issue claims; only the line number was stale. No plan impact.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      |
| D2  | Gate the spotlight (and empty-state) glow with **`withRepeat`** from `react-native-reanimated`, not `Animated.loop`.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                   | Use RN's `Animated` module per the issue's literal wording                                                                                                                           | This codebase has zero uses of RN's `Animated` API for animation; every animated component (`Confetti`, `ProgressBar`, `CardCarousel`, `BadgeEarnedModal`, `FocusPillTabBar`) uses `react-native-reanimated` (`useSharedValue`/`withTiming`/`withRepeat`). `Animated.loop` doesn't exist in that vocabulary here — the issue's phrasing is a naming slip, not a real API choice.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                            |
| D3  | `theme.chrome.badgeWallBg` is confirmed **absent** from `ChromeColors` (`packages/design-tokens/build/unistyles/semanticColors.ts:3-25`, 21 keys, none named `badgeWallBg`). D4's token risk is real today.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                            | Assume the token already exists                                                                                                                                                      | Grounds the `?? "#161616"` fallback as necessary, not defensive-programming.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                |
| D4  | The `AllThemesMatrix` precedent to follow is **`src/components/BadgeWallCell/BadgeWallCell.stories.tsx`**'s own `AllThemesMatrix` (built for #403), not `src/stories/design-system/Colors.stories.tsx` as the issue cites.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                             | Follow the issue's cited file                                                                                                                                                        | Read `Colors.stories.tsx` in full — it has no per-theme iteration at all (single active-theme swatches via `useUnistyles()`). `BadgeWallCell.stories.tsx` is the actual, already-shipped `ScopedTheme` + `themeNames` (from `themes/compose.ts`) + `MOOD_NAMES` (mirrored from `ContrastAudit.stories.tsx`) pattern — and it's the more relevant precedent anyway since `BadgeWallCell` is the exact component `BadgesWall`'s gallery composes.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                             |
| D5  | On-dark foreground content uses **`palette.white`** (primary text: spotlight goal title, empty-state title) and **`palette.gray400`** (muted text: overline, spotlight date, empty-state body) — not `theme.colors.text`/`textMuted`. The "pop" accent (count number, spotlight border/label/arrow, header divider) uses **`theme.chrome.celebrationBg`**, not the prototype's literal `#ffe50c`/`#2563eb` or `theme.colors.accentPrimary`.                                                            | Use `theme.colors.*` / `theme.colors.accentPrimary` directly, matching the prototype's blue divider                                                                                  | `theme.colors.*` are calibrated for each theme's own (light, in 6 of 7 product themes) background — not for this component's fixed dark surface. Concretely: `light-highContrast`'s `accentPrimary` is literal `#000000` (`packages/design-tokens/build/unistyles/variants.ts`) — black-on-`#161616` would be invisible. `theme.chrome.celebrationBg` already varies sensibly per variant (`#ffe50c` default/highContrast/lowVision, `#f0c43a` dyslexia-warm, `#d5c88a` autismFriendly-muted, `#fdf6cc` lowInfo-pale) and is verified >9:1 contrast against `#161616` in every case — reuse it instead of inventing a new token. `palette.*` references are not raw-hex literals for lint purposes (matches existing `BadgeWallCell.styles.ts` `color: palette.white` and `Confetti.tsx` `palette.blue600` etc.) — though note (D5a) the `no-raw-colors` ESLint rule only scans `**/*.styles.ts` under `/components/`, not `/screens/`, so this file wouldn't be auto-flagged either way; `palette.*` is still the right craft choice and what the `AllThemesMatrix` visual check verifies. |
| D6  | `BadgeWallCell`'s real prop shape is `{ badge: { title: string; design: BadgeDesign \| null }; onPress: () => void; testID?: string }` — no per-item `onPress(id)`. `BadgesWall` closes over each gallery item's `id` when wiring `onPress={() => onOpenBadge(item.id)}`.                                                                                                                                                                                                                              | Assume `BadgeWallCell` takes an `id`/`onPress(id)` pair                                                                                                                              | Read `BadgeWallCell.tsx` directly; confirms the minimal presentational-subset prop already documented in the #403 plan (D4 there).                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                          |
| D7  | `BadgesWall` props accept **already-resolved** data: `design: BadgeDesign \| null` (parsed) and `spotlight.earnedAt: string \| null` (a single resolved ISO date string). JSON-parsing (`parseBadgeDesign`, `src/badges/types.ts:289`) and the `completedAt ?? createdAt` fallback stay the container's (#405) job. `BadgesWall` formats `earnedAt` for display internally via `formatDate(earnedAt, i18n.language)` (`src/utils/format.ts:15`) since it already depends on `useTranslation` for copy. | Pass the raw query row through and parse inside `BadgesWall`                                                                                                                         | Matches the "pure, prop-driven" contract in the issue and the `GoalsCockpit` precedent (accepts already-computed `progress`/`stepsCompleted`, not raw DB rows). Keeps `BadgesWall` decoupled from the Evolu row type, matching `BadgeWallCell`'s existing minimal-subset convention (D6 of #403's plan).                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                    |
| D8  | Empty state is a **bespoke inline glyph + `Text` + `Button`** composition (mirrors `GoalsCockpit`'s `TargetIcon` reticle), not the generic `EmptyState` component that `BadgesScreen.tsx` currently uses.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              | Reuse `<EmptyState icon="..." title="..." body="..." action={...} />`                                                                                                                | `EmptyState`'s `icon` prop is a single `<Text>` glyph (e.g. an emoji), not an SVG motif. The issue explicitly asks for a "ghost / outline-badge motif," matching how `GoalsCockpit` shipped its own bespoke `react-native-svg` reticle instead of `EmptyState` for the same reason (#381 Discovery Log, 2026-06-28: "bespoke empty state... concentric-circle reticle... replacing the generic `EmptyState`").                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              |
| D9  | Empty-state CTA copy embeds the trailing arrow directly in the translated string: `"See my goals →"`.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  | Add a trailing-icon slot to `Button`                                                                                                                                                 | `Button`'s `icon` prop is leading-only. Existing precedent already embeds trailing arrows in translated copy: `completion:viewJourney` = `"View Your Journey →"`, `badgeDetail:...header` = `"...SWIPE →"`. No component change needed.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                     |
| D10 | The empty-state ghost-badge glow is gated by `animationPref === "full"` identically to the spotlight glow, even though the issue's Acceptance list only calls out the spotlight.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                       | Leave the empty-state glow unconditional                                                                                                                                             | Both prototypes (`Goals + Badges C Prototype.dc.html` and `App Shell.dc.html`) apply the same looping glow animation to the empty-state ghost badge as to the spotlight card. `CLAUDE.md`'s ND accessibility contract and the existing `useAnimationPref` convention (`Confetti`, `ProgressBar`) don't allow an ungated decorative loop anywhere in the app; resolving this consistently avoids shipping one gated and one ungated loop by omission.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                        |
| D11 | `BadgeWallCell`'s #403 circle-clip regression (mentioned in the issue as historical context) is confirmed **already fixed** in the current `BadgeWallCell.tsx` — it renders `<BadgeRenderer design={...} size={60} />` directly with no clipping `View`, and its own `AllThemesMatrix`/tests already cover per-shape rendering. Nothing further to do here; `BadgesWall` just composes the already-correct cell.                                                                                       | Re-verify/re-fix shape clipping as part of this issue                                                                                                                                | Direct read of the shipped component; the fix landed in #403/PR #418.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                       |
| D13 | The empty-state CTA is a **bespoke `Pressable`** styled with `theme.chrome.celebrationBg` bg + `celebrationFg` text + `palette.black` border + `hardMd` shadow, NOT the shared `<Button>` component (plan Step 2's original wording).                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  | Reuse `<Button>` (plan-literal / D8's "Text + Button")                                                                                                                               | Confirmed with Joe 2026-07-02. `<Button>` fills with `theme.colors.accentPrimary`, which is `#000000` in `light-highContrast` — invisible on the fixed dark `#161616` surface (the exact failure class D5 exists to prevent). The prototype's CTA is itself a celebrationBg (yellow) button, and celebrationBg is the same accent the count/spotlight already use, so a bespoke on-surface CTA is both prototype-faithful and contrast-safe in all 7 themes. `no-shared-component-reimplementation` lint disabled on it with rationale.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                     |
| D12 | `BadgesWallProps` includes an additional `onSeeGoals: () => void` prop, beyond the issue's enumerated `count`/`spotlight`/`gallery`/`onOpenBadge` list, to wire the empty-state "See my goals →" CTA.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  | Give the empty-state CTA no working callback in this layer (button renders but does nothing until the container wires it in #405); or let `BadgesWall` import `useNavigation` itself | The issue mandates a pure, nav-free component AND a redesigned empty state with a working CTA (Acceptance: "Redesigned empty state matches the prototype copy + ghost-badge motif") — those two constraints only reconcile with an explicit callback prop. Mirrors the existing `BadgesScreen.tsx` empty-state action, which today calls `navigation.getParent()...navigate("GoalsTab", ...)` — the container (#405) will wire `onSeeGoals` to the same call. **Flagged in Open Questions** since it changes the issue's literal Props list, even though the reasoning is load-bearing.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                     |

## Affected Areas

All files are **new**; nothing existing is modified except the two i18n resource files below (and the i18n auto-generator output).

- `apps/native-rd/src/screens/BadgesScreen/BadgesWall.tsx`: new pure presentational component (count header, spotlight, gallery `FlatList`, empty state, animation-gated glow).
- `apps/native-rd/src/screens/BadgesScreen/BadgesWall.styles.ts`: new Unistyles stylesheet for the above.
- `apps/native-rd/src/screens/BadgesScreen/BadgesWall.stories.tsx`: new CSF3 stories — `Populated`, `SingleBadge`, `Empty`, `AllThemesMatrix`.
- `apps/native-rd/src/screens/BadgesScreen/__tests__/BadgesWall.test.tsx`: new unit tests.
- `apps/native-rd/src/i18n/resources/en/badges.json`: add `wall.count`, `wall.allVerifiable`, `wall.justEarned`; update `empty.title`, `empty.body`, `empty.action` to the redesigned copy.
- `apps/native-rd/src/i18n/resources/_register/badges.yml`: register the new/changed keys with the existing badge-voice notes (matter-of-fact, "Badge" stays the English loanword).
- `apps/native-rd/src/i18n/resources/pseudo/badges.json`: regenerated via `bun run gen:pseudo` (auto-generated diff — do not hand-edit).
- **Not touched**: `apps/native-rd/src/i18n/resources/de/badges.json` (CI-synced via `i18n-sync`); `apps/native-rd/src/screens/BadgesScreen/BadgesScreen.tsx` (container wiring is #405).

## Implementation Plan

### Step 1: i18n — wall + redesigned empty-state copy

**Files**: `src/i18n/resources/en/badges.json`, `src/i18n/resources/_register/badges.yml`, `src/i18n/resources/pseudo/badges.json` (generated)
**Commit**: `feat(native-rd): add BadgesWall i18n keys and redesigned empty state copy`
**Changes**:

- [x] Add to `en/badges.json`: `wall.count` (`"{{count}} badges"`), `wall.allVerifiable` (`"A WALL OF PROOF · ALL VERIFIABLE"`), `wall.justEarned` (`"JUST EARNED"`).
- [x] Update `empty.title` → `"Your first badge is waiting"`, `empty.body` → `"Finish a goal and you'll design a badge to mark it — a verifiable credential that's yours to keep."`, `empty.action` → `"See my goals →"`.
- [x] Do **not** add a `wall.moreCount` key (D9 of #381 / this issue's explicit instruction).
- [x] Register all new/changed keys in `_register/badges.yml` under the existing badge-voice notes (matter-of-fact tone; "Badge" stays untranslated in German — note is informational for the `de/` sync, not something this PR edits).
- [x] Run `bun run gen:pseudo` and stage **only** `pseudo/badges.json` (mirrors the #381 Discovery Log lesson: a prior PR shipped new `en/` keys without regenerating pseudo and broke `locale-parity.test.ts`).

### Step 2: `BadgesWall` presentational component + styles

**Files**: `src/screens/BadgesScreen/BadgesWall.tsx`, `src/screens/BadgesScreen/BadgesWall.styles.ts`
**Commit**: `feat(native-rd): add BadgesWall presentational view`
**Changes**:

- [x] Props: `count: number`, `spotlight: { id: string; design: BadgeDesign | null; goalTitle: string; earnedAt: string | null } | null`, `gallery: { id: string; design: BadgeDesign | null; title: string }[]`, `onOpenBadge: (id: string) => void`, `onSeeGoals: () => void` (D12). No `useQuery`/`useNavigation` import. (Named exports `BadgesWallSpotlight`/`BadgesWallGalleryItem`/`BadgesWallProps`.)
- [x] Early-return the empty state when `count === 0` (mirrors `GoalsCockpit`'s `if (!hero)` / old `BadgesScreen`'s `if (rows.length === 0)` pattern) — bespoke ghost-badge SVG glyph + `Text` (title/body) + **bespoke celebrationBg `Pressable` CTA** (`badges:empty.action`, D9's embedded arrow) wired to the `onSeeGoals` prop (D12). CTA is bespoke, not the shared `<Button>` — see **D13**.
- [x] Header: count in `theme.chrome.celebrationBg` (large, `fontFamily.headline`), overline `badges:wall.allVerifiable` in `palette.gray400` DM-Mono caps. Header bottom border uses `theme.chrome.celebrationBg` (D5) instead of the prototype's literal blue.
- [x] Spotlight card (rendered only when `spotlight` is non-null): `theme.chrome.celebrationBg`-bordered card, `BadgeRenderer`-backed art (inline `SpotlightArt`, no nested `Pressable`), `badges:wall.justEarned` label + `spotlight.goalTitle` + `formatDate(spotlight.earnedAt, i18n.language)`, trailing `→` glyph, all in `theme.chrome.celebrationBg`/`palette.white`/`palette.gray400` per D5. `Pressable` → `onOpenBadge(spotlight.id)`.
- [x] Gallery: `FlatList` with `numColumns` (computed from `useWindowDimensions` + `CELL_SIZE` + `GALLERY_GAP`, floored at 3), `data={gallery}`, `keyExtractor={(item) => item.id}`, `renderItem` → `<BadgeWallCell badge={{ title: item.title, design: item.design }} onPress={() => onOpenBadge(item.id)} testID={...} />`. **No cap, no "+N MORE" footer** (D9). Header + spotlight ride in `ListHeaderComponent` so the whole surface scrolls/virtualizes together.
- [x] Wall surface bg: `#161616` literal directly (NOT `theme.chrome.badgeWallBg ?? …` — that key doesn't exist on the strict `ChromeColors` type and wouldn't type-check; see Discovery Log D3-refinement) with the `// TOKEN-RISK` comment (do not pre-author the token — D3).
- [x] Spotlight + empty-state glow: `useAnimationPref()` → `if (animationPref === "full") { glow.value = withRepeat(withTiming(1, …), -1, true); } else { glow.value = 0; }` inside a `useEffect`; the `useAnimatedStyle` maps `full` → breathing opacity 0.35↔1, else 0. Shared single glow value used by both the spotlight halo and the empty-state ghost halo (D2, D10).
- [x] Zero hardcoded hex in this file/styles file except the one documented `#161616` fallback (D3, D5). (`no-shared-component-reimplementation` disables added for the bespoke CTA + tappable spotlight card, with rationale.)
- [x] 44×44pt touch targets + `accessibilityRole`/`accessibilityLabel` on the spotlight card and the empty-state CTA (gallery cells already carry their own a11y contract via `BadgeWallCell`).

### Step 3: `BadgesWall` stories incl. `AllThemesMatrix`

**Files**: `src/screens/BadgesScreen/BadgesWall.stories.tsx`
**Commit**: `feat(native-rd): add BadgesWall stories incl. AllThemesMatrix`
**Changes**:

- [x] `Populated`: `spotlight` set + 15 `gallery` items (mixed shapes, mirroring `BadgeWallCell.stories.tsx`'s `ROW_BADGES` shape variety).
- [x] `SingleBadge`: `count: 1`, `spotlight` set, `gallery: []`.
- [x] `Empty`: `count: 0`, `spotlight: null`, `gallery: []`.
- [x] `AllThemesMatrix`: iterate `themeNames` (from `themes/compose.ts`) inside `ScopedTheme`, labeled with the same `MOOD_NAMES` map used in `BadgeWallCell.stories.tsx` (7 columns) — render the wall surface with a spotlight + gallery cells per column (D4).
- [x] Ran the 7-theme check: built `storybook:web:build` (clean) and served the static build, screenshotting `AllThemesMatrix` (all 7 themes), `Populated`, and `Empty`. **Outcome: PASS — `#161616` reads correctly in all 7 themes; celebrationBg accent stays legible (yellow in default/dark/highContrast/lowVision, warm gold in dyslexia, muted gold in autismFriendly, pale cream in lowInfo). No theme needed a lighter/warmer dark surface → conditional Step 5 NOT triggered.** (See Discovery Log.)

### Step 4: `BadgesWall` unit tests

**Files**: `src/screens/BadgesScreen/__tests__/BadgesWall.test.tsx`
**Commit**: `test(native-rd): add BadgesWall unit tests`
**Changes**:

- [x] Count header renders `t("badges:wall.count", { count })`.
- [x] Spotlight renders exactly the `spotlight` prop's title/date/art when non-null; absent when `spotlight` is null; date row omitted when `earnedAt` is null.
- [x] Gallery renders one cell per `gallery` item (`test.each` over `0/1/20`) and never renders a "+N MORE"/"MORE" string. (FlatList rendered all 20 in the jsdom env — the 20-exact assertion holds.)
- [x] Empty state (`count: 0`) renders the redesigned copy (`badges:empty.title/body/action`) and calls `onSeeGoals` on press; count header + spotlight are absent.
- [x] Animation gating: mocked `useAnimationPref` `"full"` → `withRepeat` called; `"reduced"`/`"none"` → not called (local reanimated mock exposes `withRepeat` as a `jest.fn`).
- [x] Pseudo-locale bracketed-copy check for the new `wall.*` keys and updated `empty.*` keys (mirrors the existing `BadgesScreen.test.tsx` "pseudo locale" describe block).
- [x] `BadgeRenderer`/`BadgeWallCell` internals are exercised via the existing `BadgeWallCell.test.tsx` — not re-mocked here. **21 tests, all green.**

### Step 5 (conditional): file the `badgeWall` surface-token issue

**Only if** Step 3's 7-theme check shows `#161616` doesn't adapt (e.g. a variant needs a lighter/warmer dark, per the issue's own note on dyslexia-cream/autismFriendly-muted). Not a commit in this PR — file a new GitHub issue (`badgeWall` per-theme surface token, referencing `packages/design-tokens/src/tokens/chrome.json` + the relevant theme JSON overrides, following the exact pattern used for #376/`screen-header-bg`) **before closing #404**. Do not pre-author the token (per issue instruction and D3).

**Outcome: NOT triggered.** The 7-theme visual check (Step 3) confirmed `#161616` reads correctly in every product theme and the celebrationBg accent stays legible on it in all 7 — no lighter/warmer dark needed. No issue filed; the `// TOKEN-RISK` comment stays in `BadgesWall.styles.ts` as a pointer for a future designer if that ever changes.

## Testing Strategy

- [ ] Unit tests for `BadgesWall`: count header, spotlight = prop, gallery renders all (no cap), empty state, animation gating, pseudo-locale parity (Jest 30, `@testing-library/react-native` v13, `bun run test --testPathPatterns BadgesWall`).
- [ ] Test file lives at `src/screens/BadgesScreen/__tests__/BadgesWall.test.tsx`, mirroring the existing `src/` structure.
- [ ] Use `test.each`/`describe.each` for repetitive cases (gallery sizes, animation-pref values, pseudo-locale keys) — matches `BadgeWallCell.test.tsx` and `BadgesScreen.test.tsx` conventions.
- [ ] `bun run gen:pseudo` re-run after any `en/badges.json` change, and `locale-parity.test.ts` must stay green (it only diffs `en` vs `pseudo`; `de/` is out of scope for this PR).
- [ ] Manual/visual: `bun run storybook:web`, open `Badges/BadgesWall`, verify `Populated`/`SingleBadge`/`Empty` states and step through all 7 themes via the toolbar or `AllThemesMatrix` — this **is** the design-check gate (green tests don't substitute for it).
- [ ] `bun run storybook:web:build` builds clean.

## Not in Scope

| Item                                                                               | Reason                                                                                    | Follow-up             |
| ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | --------------------- |
| `BadgesScreen.tsx` container wiring (`useQuery`, navigation, mapping rows → props) | Explicitly deferred to #405 per the issue body                                            | #405                  |
| Sort/filter controls, hamburger menu                                               | Mockup-only, out of scope per #381 Not-in-Scope and this issue's explicit instruction     | None filed            |
| `wall.moreCount` key / "+N MORE" cap                                               | D9 — density is the point; `FlatList` virtualizes                                         | None filed            |
| `badgeWall` per-theme surface token pre-authored                                   | Explicit instruction: discover during implementation, only add if the 7-theme check fails | Conditional Step 5    |
| Re-fixing `BadgeWallCell`'s circle-clip regression                                 | Already fixed in the shipped #403/PR #418 component (D11)                                 | None — verified fixed |
| Bottom-nav / App Shell redesign                                                    | Separate epic tracks (#379 and others)                                                    | #379                  |

## Open Questions

- **`onSeeGoals` prop (D12) — RESOLVED 2026-07-02 (Joe):** add `onSeeGoals: () => void` as a **required** prop, sibling to `onOpenBadge`. `BadgesWall` stays pure/nav-free; #405's container wires it to the same Goals-tab navigate the current `BadgesScreen` empty-state action already performs. Stories pass a Storybook action. This is the chosen wiring — not optional, not deferred-inert. Step 2 and D12 already reflect this; no further open questions.

-->

- [2026-07-02] **D3 refinement:** `ComposedTheme.chrome` is typed as strict `ChromeColors` (compose.ts:83 → semanticColors.ts:3-25, 21 keys, no `badgeWallBg`). Writing `theme.chrome.badgeWallBg ?? "#161616"` as the plan's Step 2 literally states would fail `tsc` (property doesn't exist). Implemented the documented `#161616` fallback as a direct literal with the `// TOKEN-RISK` comment instead — this is exactly what D3's rationale intends (the fallback IS the value while no per-theme token exists). No behavior change vs the plan's intent.
- [2026-07-02] **D2 refinement:** `withRepeat` has zero existing usages in the codebase and is absent from the shared reanimated test mock (`src/__tests__/mocks/reanimated.ts`), so a component using it would crash on render under Jest. Added `withRepeat` to that shared mock (returns its animation arg, mirroring `withTiming`/`withSpring`) so `BadgesWall` renders in tests; the BadgesWall test additionally spies on it for the animation-gating assertions. Small, additive test-infra change beyond the "all files new except i18n" scope note.
- [2026-07-02] Step 1 committed (`ef6089f`): i18n keys + redesigned empty-state copy. `gen:pseudo` regenerated all pseudo files but only `pseudo/badges.json` had content changes relevant to this PR; reverted 4 unrelated files (badgeDetail/completion/editGoal/focusMode) that showed only pre-existing trailing-`·` padding drift. `locale-parity` (20) + `syncCore` (6) green. (The `_register/badges.yml` notes use folded block scalars `>-` because plain multi-line scalars broke prettier's YAML parser on `: ` sequences.)
- [2026-07-02] **D13 (empty-state CTA) — RESOLVED (Joe):** asked mid-implementation because the plan's Step 2 said "use `<Button>`" but the prototype renders a celebrationBg (yellow) CTA and `<Button>`'s `accentPrimary` fill is `#000000` (invisible on `#161616`) in highContrast. Joe chose the **bespoke celebrationBg `Pressable`**. See D13.
- [2026-07-02] Step 2 committed (`7076142`): `BadgesWall.tsx` + `BadgesWall.styles.ts`. Step 3 committed (`246f6fc`): stories incl. `AllThemesMatrix`. Step 4 committed (`687e621`): 21 unit tests + `withRepeat` added to the shared reanimated mock.
- [2026-07-02] **Visual gate (Step 3) — PASS.** Built `storybook:web:build` clean; served the static build and screenshotted `AllThemesMatrix` (all 7 themes side by side), `Empty`, and `Populated`. `#161616` reads correctly in all 7 themes; celebrationBg accent legible in every one; spotlight badge keeps its own shape (no circle-clip); dense gallery renders all cells with distinct shapes; empty state matches the prototype (ghost badge + copy + yellow CTA). Conditional Step 5 not triggered.
- [2026-07-02] Final validation: `type-check` PASS, `lint` PASS (0 errors; only pre-existing repo-wide warnings, none in the new files), full test suite **9523 passed / 193 suites**, `build` no-op PASS.
